content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Closed the remaining lifecycle amendments. `run.pid` now carries the owning writer-lock id, CLI/daemon run launches record that owner in the pid file, and terminal engine paths keep `run.pid` in place until the caller successfully releases the writer lease/worktree lease. `run resume` can now reclaim a fresh stranded CLI cleanup lock when the matching pid record proves the owning orchestrator is dead, fixing the post-run cleanup crash window without stealing unrelated live CLI work. Separately, pid records backed only by non-Linux `proc_start_marker` are no longer treated as authoritative for liveness or stop decisions, eliminating the unsafe same-second PID-reuse path. Added regression coverage for fresh cleanup-lock recovery and the stricter pid-authority rule.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Extended pid persistence in `src/adapters/fs.rs` to record the owning writer lease id, made authoritative liveness require Linux-style start ticks, and tightened pid helpers/tests so `proc_start_marker` is retained for compatibility but no longer trusted for stop/resume decisions.
2. [Completed] Updated `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` so CLI/daemon launches write pid files with the writer-owner id, terminal engine paths leave `run.pid` in place until their caller finishes lease cleanup, and direct engine-only wrappers still remove pid files on return for test-only execution paths.
3. [Completed] Patched `src/cli/run.rs` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` so successful CLI/daemon cleanup removes `run.pid` only after releasing the relevant lock, and `run resume` can reclaim a fresh stranded CLI cleanup lock only when the observed lock owner matches a dead authoritative pid record for that same owner.
4. [Completed] Added/updated regressions in `tests/cli.rs` and adapter/engine unit coverage for fresh failed-snapshot cleanup-lock recovery and non-authoritative start-marker pid records, then reran formatting, targeted checks, the full test suite, and clippy.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_resume_reclaims_writer_lock_for_failed_snapshot_cleanup_crash -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test pid_record_with_only_start_marker_is_not_authoritative` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` failed only on pre-existing repo-wide lint findings outside this lifecycle amendment.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` is still blocked by pre-existing repository-wide lint debt outside this change set, including findings in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs`.
- On non-Linux Unix platforms, current-version pid files no longer provide authoritative liveness proof, so fresh post-run cleanup-lock recovery falls back to lease staleness there until a stronger per-process identity is available.
- The worktree already had unrelated live-state changes under `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/`; those files were left untouched.
